extracted_sentences: Conveniently located on every street corner, McDonald’s is open 24/7 ready to nuke up a Happy Meal at all times.
First of all, Burger King doesn’t get their coffee to that tongue-scorching hot temperature we’ve all come to love and endure.
I like to hear the crunch of the lettuce when biting into a burger and McDonald’s doesn’t provide that satisfaction with any of their many boring burgers.
McDonald’s is best known for its breakfast menu; however, they stop serving outrageously early.
Secondly, Burger King’s hash brown bites taste as if they forgot to add an ingredient; I call the missing ingredient taste.

baseline_scores.sentence: During this evaluation period, I continued working as a Data Analyst in the Quality Department at NOX US, with an expanded focus on data engineering, system optimization, and backend-oriented development in addition to data analysis.

While my official role remains Data Analyst, my responsibilities have expanded to include system-level contributions such as backend support, database optimization, and data infrastructure improvements.
My core responsibility remained analyzing product defect data to identify root causes and recommend corrective actions based on internal data patterns.
During this period, I improved my ability to work with larger and more complex datasets by developing more efficient data processing workflows using Python and database-integrated tools.
Compared to the previous evaluation period, I strengthened my technical proficiency in Python, SQL, Power BI, TypeScript, HTML, and CSS.
I became more involved in writing queries, improving database structures, and enhancing data flow between systems, which allowed me to contribute beyond analysis into system-level improvements.
I contributed to enhancing internal web-based applications that store, visualize, and export operational data.
This included improving performance, data accessibility, and usability for both technical and non-technical users.
I also worked on converting unstructured historical Excel data into a structured database, improving data consistency and long-term maintainability.
Additionally, I supported IT-related functions such as equipment management, testing new systems, and troubleshooting technical issues.
I participated in efforts to modernize equipment systems, including supporting IoT-related upgrades, which helped me better understand real-time data integration and system interactions.
Throughout this period, I focused on improving scalability, efficiency, and usability of data systems.
I developed a stronger understanding of backend infrastructure and system design, enabling me to contribute to data architecture and system integration efforts.
There have been no major changes to the original objectives outlined in the training plan.
However, my responsibilities have expanded in scope and technical depth, particularly in areas related to backend systems, data engineering, and system design.
Overall, this evaluation period reflects significant growth in both technical capability and the ability to contribute to scalable, data-driven solutions that support organizational decision-making.

no_fewshot_rewrites.original: During this period, I improved my ability to work with larger and more complex datasets by developing more efficient data processing workflows using Python and database-integrated tools.
no_fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: In that time, I got pretty good at wrangling large, messy datasets — mostly by writing faster processing pipelines in Python and wiring them up to various database tools.

no_fewshot_rewrites.original: Throughout this period, I focused on improving scalability, efficiency, and usability of data systems.
no_fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: For the most part, my work during that period revolved around squeezing more speed out of data systems, making them easier to grow, and ensuring the people who depended on them could actually get what they needed.

no_fewshot_rewrites.original: There have been no major changes to the original objectives outlined in the training plan.
no_fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: For the most part, the training plan's original objectives are holding up — no major changes have crept in.

no_fewshot_rewrites.original: This included improving performance, data accessibility, and usability for both technical and non-technical users.
no_fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: The aim was to speed things up, lower the barrier to entry, and build something that people without a data background could genuinely use.

no_fewshot_rewrites.original: However, my responsibilities have expanded in scope and technical depth, particularly in areas related to backend systems, data engineering, and system design.
no_fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: At this point, my work has spilled pretty far outside its original scope — backend systems, data pipelines, and the unglamorous chaos of system design have all become part of the day-to-day.

fewshot_rewrites.original: During this period, I improved my ability to work with larger and more complex datasets by developing more efficient data processing workflows using Python and database-integrated tools.
fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: During this time, I learned how to tackle massive, headache-inducing datasets by building smarter workflows with Python and a few database tools that actually did what they were supposed to do.

fewshot_rewrites.original: Throughout this period, I focused on improving scalability, efficiency, and usability of data systems.
fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: During this time, I spent my days making data systems faster, smarter, and believe it or not, actually usable by the people who had to suffer through them.

fewshot_rewrites.original: There have been no major changes to the original objectives outlined in the training plan.
fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: The training plan hasn't changed one bit since we first jotted down those original objectives on a napkin; apparently, evolution is too much to ask for.

fewshot_rewrites.original: This included improving performance, data accessibility, and usability for both technical and non-technical users.
fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: This meant picking up the pace, making the data actually findable, and ensuring even your most clueless users could figure it out without throwing their computer out the window.

fewshot_rewrites.original: However, my responsibilities have expanded in scope and technical depth, particularly in areas related to backend systems, data engineering, and system design.
fewshot_rewrites.rewritten: However, my job has somehow spiraled into a full-blown backend disaster, complete with data engineering headaches and system design decisions nobody bothered to warn me about.

sentences.sentence: Sample Student A
Professor Bradford
English M02
3 April 2013
The Clown Vs. the Crown
In the battle between McDonald’s and Burger King, scientists have devoted their lives to create a cheap, addictive source of sustenance better known as fast food.
For those who can’t afford a nice sit down restaurant, or are simply too hung-over and impatient to wait around for a hot meal, I recommend these fine eateries.
Conveniently located on every street corner, McDonald’s is open 24/7 ready to nuke up a Happy Meal at all times.
McDonald’s is best known for its breakfast menu; however, they stop serving outrageously early.
So, if time is an issue or lunch is what you desire, wave good-bye to McDonald’s as you pull into Burger King’s drive through.
Burger King’s Whopper and other burger masterpieces put the Big Mac to shame.
McDonald’s might dominate with its cheap yet satisfying breakfast, but Burger King rules over the lunch crowd.
The McDonald’s breakfast blends perfectly together to give you that short satisfied feeling that slowly morphs into regret.
A piping hot cup of coffee, golden crisp has browns, and a freshly heated sausage egg McMuffin sum up the American Dream.
Burger King’s weak attempt to compete with McDonald’s out of this world breakfast fails on all fronts.
First of all, Burger King doesn’t get their coffee to that tongue-scorching hot temperature we’ve all come to love and endure.
Where is the thrill in drinking coffee if there’s no risk in burning yourself?
Secondly, Burger King’s hash brown bites taste as if they forgot to add an ingredient; I call the missing ingredient taste.
Finally, Burger King’s breakfast sandwich closely mimics something you would find in a McDonald’s, just not as fulfilling.
Both establishments make a big mistake in not serving breakfast all day long; showing no love for those waking up at 11 a.m.
If you’re up early and left with the decision between McDonald’s or Burger King for breakfast, make the right choice and head over to McDonald’s.
For everybody else who is getting that late start, Burger King won’t disappoint by providing a nice greasy lunch combo.
If you’re arguing between McDonald’s or Burger king for lunch, I’ll save you from flipping a coin.
Yes, we all know of the notorious Big Mac, but when compared with Burger King’s Whopper side by side, the Big Mac doesn’t look nearly as big or exciting as the Whopper.
The fry cooks put a little more time into broiling up a whole bunch of Burger King’s finest combo specials.
Ranging from Veggie Burgers to the Triple Stacker, Burger King has something for everyone’s unique taste and demands.
The freshness of the vegetables that goes into their food is an important deciding factor.
I like to hear the crunch of the lettuce when biting into a burger and McDonald’s doesn’t provide that satisfaction with any of their many boring burgers.
A really big difference between these two places is how they prepare their fries.
McDonald’s is a gamble; sometimes their fries are crispy and decent, but more of the time they are too flimsy and heavily salted.
Fries at Burger King are cut and seasoned with perfection you won’t need a dipping sauce while shoveling them into your mouth.

McDonald’s and Burger King both have a large lunch menu, but Burger King’s superior method of broiling and using fresher ingredients to cook their products, makes Burger King hands down the better option for lunch and dinner.
Unhealthy, fast and cheap.
The world wouldn’t be the same without McDonald’s or Burger King.
Higher life expectancy, less obesity, and all around happier people would be seen a lot more often if we could just give up the devilish temptation that these eateries create within society.
But, just like the smoker who lights up a cigarette until their last breath, fast food has gotten us hooked by putting the right amount of chemicals in the food to keep us coming back for more.
The heavenly taste of a McDonald’s breakfast and the over powering flavor of Burger King’s flame broiled goodness is the work of modern science at its finest.
